level_signals: At least 3 years working on highly technical SaaS or cloud infrastructure products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years experience in product management of technical products with at least 3 years working on  highly technical SaaS or cloud infrastructure products.
at least 3 years working on  highly technical SaaS or cloud infrastructure products.
BS/MS/PhD in Computer Science or related technical field
Strong empirical, data-driven approach to deployment and decision-making.
You will drive the strategy and roadmap for core database capabilities to make transformative improvements to performance, scalability, efficiency, and search quality.
The ideal candidate is passionate about understanding developer needs of building AI applications in a highly dynamic and evolving AI landscape.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrated success in an unstructured environment
A passion for databases and/or search

scope.surface: Core vector database capabilities — performance, scalability, efficiency, and search quality
core_responsibilities: Spearhead the development of core database capabilities from concept through launch, ensuring high quality execution and alignment with the company's strategic vision.
Collaborate cross-functionally with engineering, marketing, sales, and customer success teams to ensure high quality execution, cohesive product messaging, and effective go-to-market strategies.
Collect user feedback to inform product strategy and identify new opportunities for innovation.
Define and prioritize product requirements based on target user personas and market research
Forecast timelines and communicate product strategy and deliverables to company leadership

green_flags: Clear product surface with technical depth — core database capabilities for a leading vector database
Named investors (Andreessen Horowitz, ICONIQ, Menlo Ventures, Wing Venture Capital) and disclosed funding ($138M) signal financial stability
Equity award explicitly mentioned as part of compensation
Cross-functional ownership scope is well-defined (engineering, marketing, sales, customer success)
Comprehensive benefits including mental health, parental leave, and WFH stipend
red_flags: Location type is unclear — JD does not explicitly state remote, hybrid, or on-site expectations
Hard credential requirement (BS/MS/PhD in CS) with no 'or equivalent experience' clause may exclude strong practitioners without formal CS degrees

ambiguities: Location type is not specified in the JD body — the company is 'based in New York' but whether this role is remote, hybrid, or on-site is never stated. Marked as 'unclear'.

The credential requirement 'BS/MS/PhD in Computer Science or related technical field' does not include an 'or equivalent experience' clause, which is unusual — severity kept as hard but may be negotiable in practice.
The distinction between 'Staff' and 'Principal' level is not clarified; both titles appear in the role name without differentiation in requirements.
